title: oauth2-proxy trusts X-Forwarded-* headers from any client when --reverse-proxy is enabled
description: IsProxied returns the global scope.ReverseProxy flag (set by --reverse-proxy) without validating the direct caller's address. Every X-Forwarded-* reader (GetRequestProto/Host/URI) uses IsProxied to decide whether to trust client-supplied forwarded headers, so any client can spoof X-Forwarded-Uri to make skip_auth_routes matching evaluate against a different path than the one the upstream actually serves. The fix introduces CanTrustForwardedHeaders, gated by a configured trusted-proxy IP allowlist.
